title: Boston - Khoury College of Computer Sciences
text:
Boston - Khoury College of Computer Sciences
Khoury College in Boston
A groundbreaking leader in experiential learning and research
Boston is the largest and first Northeastern campus, and where Khoury College began. It offers vast research and career opportunities on campus and with many local industry partners. The Boston campus is an excellent location to launch or accelerate your computer science career.
A hundred years of history and opportunity
Learn about how the Boston campus is at the center of interdisciplinary, groundbreaking computer science research and learning.

75 degree programs
#1 in co-ops and internships*

A leader in education, tech, and innovation
Boston is a top tech hub with a landscape of big tech firms as well as dozens of finance titans and biotech pioneers. Khoury College’s longstanding relationships with leading companies and research labs provide students and faculty access to a range of partners — big and small.
Through experiential learning at companies like Google, Facebook, Hubspot, PwC, Amazon, and many more, students apply skills from the classroom to real-world industry problems and build a network to drive their careers. And with dozens of on-site research labs, there are many opportunities to be on the cutting edge of everything from machine learning and artificial intelligence to network science and cybersecurity.

Student experience: A community of excited peers and welcoming faculty
Northeastern’s green and spacious urban campus provides a welcome place to study and connect in the heart of Boston. Dozens of Northeastern clubs provide opportunities to meet new friends and find interdisciplinary collaborators, while sports facilities, cafes, and outdoor study areas provide a social life right on campus. Faculty are ready to help after class, during office hours, and through research opportunities.
Students have free access to the Museum of Fine Arts, which is right across the street from Khoury College. The MBTA station at the center of campus provides easy access to the rest of Greater Boston, including the nearby Prudential Center and the historic districts of Beacon Hill and Back Bay.
Take a quick tour of the Boston campus
Join Khoury student Logan Stanhope as she shows you around Northeastern’s Boston campus and the many classrooms and labs where students collaborate.
Featured faculty: Saiph Savage
Saiph Savage is an assistant professor and director of the Civic A.I. Lab at Khoury College. Her research focuses on creating intelligent civic technology to organize collective action for change, which includes battling misinformation and empowering gig and rural workers to access better jobs.
